evidence.excerpt: Frozendict lookups use locks and atomic operations for thread safety. We should be able to use a faster implementation for `frozendict`, avoid locks and atomic operations, since the `frozendict` mapping is immutable.

evidence.excerpt: I think for future proofing, it is better for the `sysconfig` module not to depend on `sys.version` because it is fragile.
evidence.author: char101

nodes.object_label: sysconfig.get_platform() could return wrong value because sys.version is truncated

evidence.excerpt: sysconfig.get_platform() could return wrong value because sys.version is truncated
evidence.author: char101

nodes.label: caused_by

evidence.excerpt: On Windows, `sysconfig.get_platform()` checks for the string `amd64` (for 64 intel) in `sys.version`.
evidence.author: char101

evidence.excerpt: This is the value of `sys.version` for python 3.14.3 (64 bit) compiled with clang 22.1.0
evidence.author: char101

evidence.excerpt: This looks like an artifact of `clang` being untested & [unsupported](https://peps.python.org/pep-0011/) on Windows.
evidence.author: encukou

evidence.excerpt: The `plaform` module seems to be a better choice.
